Substantive review complete; dossier supports approval; deferred pending independent second reviewer. Original: Rank17=chicorée (fructan, burden 50x threshold) → rank16=artichaut (fructan); same subtype; -86% burden. Chicory root is commercial inulin/FOS source (fructan). Artichoke primarily fructan (Monash). Extreme source load makes this swap clinically highly valuable. Coverage 0.167 acceptable: both foods are fructan-dominant with no known polyol concern. Add UI note: extreme source load on chicory (burden 6.0 vs 0.12 threshold). Lit: Muir 2007; Monash high-low FODMAP foods page.

Substantive review complete; dossier supports approval; deferred pending independent second reviewer. Original: Rank19=lentille verte (GOS, burden 4.17) → rank21=lentille brune (GOS, burden 1.5); same subtype; -64% burden. GOS is definitively the primary FODMAP in legumes (Monash, Dysseler & Hoffem). Coverage 0.167 acceptable: lentils' other FODMAP contributors are negligible vs GOS. Analogue to approved B01_004 (green→red lentil, -66%). Lit: Monash blog legumes 2018; FODMAP Everyday lentils guide Dec 2023.

Rank7=ciboule blanc (fructan HIGH) → rank42=céleri branche (MANNITOL, moderate). Cross-subtype swap: fructan intolerant users could inadvertently ingest mannitol. Monash confirms celery is mannitol-dominant (moderate at 75g, updated Oct 2025, George Eats). Rule is clinically unsafe without explicit per-user subtype-tolerance gating. Lit: Monash mannitol foods; George Eats celery Oct 2025; Gourmend celery Nov 2025.

Rank15=orge perlé (fructan, 75g serving, burden 1.094) → rank14=seigle (fructan, 40g serving, burden 1.067). Burden improvement is -2.5% — below minimum clinically meaningful threshold. More critically: rye contains 4.4-6.6 g fructan/100g (Laatikainen et al. PMC5113694) vs barley 0.9-4.2 g/100g. At equivalent weight, rye typically carries MORE fructans than barley, not less. The apparent improvement is a serving-size artefact (different gram denominators), not a true reduction. Culinary substitution is non-obvious (barley in soups/risotto ≠ rye in bread). Lit: Laatikainen et al. PMC5113694; Biesiekierski 2011; ScienceDirect 2020.

Substantive review complete; dossier supports approval; deferred pending independent second reviewer. Original: Rank13=farine T80 (fructan, burden 1.467) → rank12=farine T65 (fructan, burden 0.933); same subtype; -36% burden. Fructan concentrates in bran (Biesiekierski 2011). T80 has more bran than T65; lower T-number = less bran = less fructan. Scientifically sound: ScienceDirect 2020 confirms refined wheat flours suitable for low-FODMAP vs wholemeal. Same food family (wheat flour), valid culinary substitution. Coverage 0.167 acceptable: wheat flour's only FODMAP concern is fructan (Monash, Biesiekierski 2011). Lit: Biesiekierski et al. 2011 J Hum Nutr Diet; ScienceDirect doi:10.1016/j.jcs.2020.103004.
